title: add timeout support for image copy
body:
the copy sub command may stuck if there are some network issue(e.g. slow network cause of network flapping in infrastructure), skopeo stuck there and application cannot do anything. It results in all the workers hang and cannot continue(retry, or report failure). 

Add the command-timeout option to make it possible to terminate the command to avoid this case.

Test result:
```
./skopeo copy --command-timeout=2s  --src-tls-verify=true --src-creds ****:***** docker://ubuntu:14.04 dir:/tmp/*****/raw
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:*******
 4.53 MB / 48.98 MB [=====>-------------------------------------------------] 1s
FATA[0002] Error writing blob: context deadline exceeded
```

comments.body: This would require modifications of man pages, command completions.
@rhatdan comments addressed. please take a look

Thanks!

The ability to timeout at all is certainly useful, OTOH if your whole ”worker” can get stuck on `skopeo` and will hang indefinitely, arguably the timeout should be at the worker level, not at the level of `skopeo`.

There’s also an universal `timeout`(1) command, so this option is not _strictly_ necessary, though this PR produces _much_ nicer error reports:
```console
$ LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 timeout -v 1s sleep 10
timeout: sending signal TERM to command ‘sleep’
```

Adding this option does not hurt, certainly, beyond the small long-time maintenance burden, but I just wanted to make sure you have considered the two alternatives above.

@mtrmac 
Thank you for your feedback. Agree that there are other options to get the hang issue resolved, it would be nicer to get `skopeo` client support `command-timeout`.   With the option, operator can directly get to know what is the problem and resolve the issue with less effort. 

like the etcd client - `etcdctl` - has the command option. it allows operator to get the command to run with regular options. e.g.
```
etcdctl defrag --command-timeout=20s --cacert=.... --server=...
```

Fair enough.  Then, two design questions:

- Is 30 minutes (or any timeout at all, really) a reasonable default? It’s not obviously unreasonable, at least.
- Should this be an option specific to `skopeo copy`, or a global option applicable to all subcommands? The latter seems a bit preferable.

@rhatdan , others, WDYT?
@mtrmac . make sense. will update the code to add it into global command option.

@mtrmac the option has been moved to global option and change the default value to 0 and have the default infinite time.
cc @rhatdan

Yes I think the default should be infinite.
LGTM
